level_signals: 3-4 years of experience in project management, consulting, strategy & operations
Senior Associate title with cross-functional influence but no direct reports mentioned

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3-4 years of experience in project management, consulting, strategy & operations, preferably within a high-growth technology, SaaS, or hospitality environment.
must_haves.qualifier: project management, consulting, or strategy & operations

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You take ownership of projects, work autonomously, and demonstrate initiative to drive change. You are skilled at identifying issues, assessing risks, and finding creative, pragmatic solutions. You complete day-to-day tasks without instruction and require only general guidance on new or more complex types of work.

Proven track record of managing initiatives of medium complexity that require a high level of accuracy and dependability. You can manage multiple priorities simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.

You come to meetings with a clear point of view and feel comfortable pushing back and asking hard questions. You are personable, capable of influencing others, and skilled at bringing people together to drive results.
An analytical mindset with proven experience in data analysis and financial modeling. You can translate raw data into actionable insights and strategic recommendations.
Financial Modeling Experience: The ability to create financial models to evaluate potential opportunities.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and align cross-functional teams without direct authority.
You have strong data analysis skills and high spreadsheet comfort. Experience with Salesforce, Looker, and Gong is highly preferred.
Customer Centric: Deep sense of empathy for user challenges and pain points.
You have a Bachelor's Degree in a related field; Masters Degree preferred but not required.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Industry Enthusiast: Experience in hospitality tech, a consumer-facing app, or a B2B2C environment is a nice-to-have.
You have a Bachelor's Degree in a related field; Masters Degree preferred but not required.
3-4 years of experience in project management, consulting, strategy & operations, preferably within a high-growth technology, SaaS, or hospitality environment.

scope.surface: In-Store business line (SevenRooms); new add-on product commercialization (Events, Lite) from MVP through global GTM launch
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS (hospitality tech / restaurant management platform)
primary_metrics_inferred: KPIs and dashboards tracking competitive dynamics
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end commercialization of new add-on products globally (MVP to full GTM launch)
Act as cross-functional 'train conductor' across Product, Sales, Marketing, and Legal
Size market opportunities and conduct competitive research
Dive deep into data to uncover trends, analyze product adoption, and monitor competitive dynamics
Develop dashboards, KPIs, and reports translating data into actionable recommendations
Translate ambiguous project scopes into structured, actionable plans and communicate to senior leadership
Anticipate and proactively manage risks and obstacles to strategic projects
Manage legal terms, product naming, FAQs, sales positioning, pricing and packaging strategies

green_flags: Comprehensive benefits package detailed (401k, parental leave, healthcare)
High executive visibility role with clear scope and ownership
Clear product surface (SevenRooms In-Store) rather than vague mandate
red_flags: Work location type not specified — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite
Pay range is broad ($87K-$128K), suggesting significant location-based variation without clarity on how it applies
JD lists 'Product' twice in the cross-functional partners list ('Product, Sales, Marketing, Product, Sales, and Legal') — minor inconsistency suggesting JD may not have been carefully proofread
Role requires managing global GTM launches with regional nuance but no mention of international experience as a requirement

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, years, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from SevenRooms context and B2B SaaS mention in JD)
ambiguities: Work location is not specified anywhere in the JD body — could be onsite, hybrid, or remote; marked as 'unclear'

The 'Salesforce, Looker, and Gong' requirement is described as 'highly preferred' which could be either hard or soft; treated as hard given the phrasing 'highly preferred' in an otherwise unconditional bullet

The SaaS/hospitality industry preference appears both in the hard experience bullet (3-4 years 'preferably within') and is partially captured as a nice-to-have; dual-captured to reflect the JD's mixed signaling
validation_issues: must_have autonomous_ownership: raw_text_from_jd not found in JD even after bullet/whitespace normalization
